description: Held-out synthesis questions. 15 tasks, 5 per shape (agg / filter / granular). Designed so a generic snippet-skim baseline cannot reach the answer in a single search: every question requires either list-then-lookup-per-item-then-aggregate or list-then-filter-by-non-canonical-property. Replaces the prior factoid eval set, which saturated baseline at 100% even after a deliberate difficulty rewrite (see writeup/notes.md). Never shown to the stem during evolution.

shapes.filter: Filter axis differs from list axis: canonical list, non-canonical filter.
shapes.granular: At least one ingredient too granular/recent/obscure for parametric memory.

tasks.question: Of the current 32 NATO member countries (as of June 2024), how many joined NATO in the year 2000 or later?
tasks.notes: 1999 wave (CZ, HU, PL) excluded. 2004: BG, EE, LV, LT, RO, SK, SI (7). 2009: AL, HR (2). 2017: ME (1). 2020: MK (1). 2023: FI (1). 2024: SE (1). Total 13.

tasks.question: Of the 6 official languages of the United Nations, how many use a script other than the Latin alphabet?
tasks.notes: UN official languages: Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, Spanish. Non-Latin: Arabic (Arabic script), Chinese (Han), Russian (Cyrillic). Latin: English, French, Spanish.

tasks.question: Of the 5 oceans of Earth, how many have a maximum depth exceeding 7,000 meters?
tasks.notes: Pacific (Mariana ~10,911m), Atlantic (Puerto Rico Trench ~8,376m), Indian (Sunda ~7,290m), Southern (South Sandwich ~7,236m). Arctic (~5,550m) is the only one below 7,000m.

tasks.question: Of the 7 New Wonders of the World (announced by the New 7 Wonders Foundation in 2007), how many are located in countries in the Western Hemisphere?
tasks.notes: Christ the Redeemer (Brazil), Machu Picchu (Peru), Chichen Itza (Mexico). The other 4 (Great Wall, Petra, Colosseum, Taj Mahal) are in the Eastern Hemisphere. Trivia-listicle risk; included as the 'easy' end of the agg shape.

tasks.question: Of the 32 teams in the NFL (as of the 2023 regular season), how many have never won a Super Bowl?
tasks.notes: Never-won as of end of 2023 season: Vikings, Bills, Browns, Bengals, Lions, Cardinals, Falcons, Chargers, Texans, Panthers, Titans, Jaguars.

tasks.question: Of the 10 most populous countries in the world (per 2024 UN estimates), how many have at least one official language whose primary script is something other than the Latin alphabet?
tasks.notes: Top 10 (2024): India (Devanagari, Bengali), China (Han), USA (Latin), Indonesia (Latin), Pakistan (Urdu/Arabic-derived script), Nigeria (Latin), Brazil (Latin), Bangladesh (Bengali), Russia (Cyrillic), Mexico (Latin). Non-Latin: India, China, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Russia = 5. Filter axis (script) is independent of list axis (population).

tasks.question: Of the 8 unique countries that have hosted the FIFA Women's World Cup from 1991 through 2023 inclusive (treating co-hosts as separate countries), how many have also hosted a FIFA Men's World Cup at any point?
tasks.notes: Women's hosts (unique countries): China, Sweden, USA, Germany, Canada, France, Australia, New Zealand. Of these, men's hosts: Sweden (1958), USA (1994), Germany (1974/2006), France (1938/1998) = 4.

tasks.question: Of the 10 most populous countries in the world (per 2024 UN estimates), how many have a Muslim-majority population (more than 50% Muslim)?
tasks.notes: Indonesia, Pakistan, Bangladesh. Nigeria is roughly evenly split and most demographic surveys put Muslims slightly under 50% nationally; excluded. Filter axis (religion) independent of list axis (population).

tasks.question: Of the 12 founding members of NATO (signatories of the 1949 North Atlantic Treaty), how many had a hereditary monarch as their head of state at the time of founding?
tasks.notes: Founding 12: USA, UK, France, Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Italy, Norway, Denmark, Iceland, Portugal, Canada. Monarchies in 1949: UK (George VI), Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg, Norway, Denmark, plus Canada (Commonwealth realm with George VI as head of state) = 7. Iceland (republic 1944), Italy (republic 1946), France, Portugal, USA were republics.

tasks.question: Of the 12 official languages of South Africa (as recognized after the 2023 constitutional amendment that added South African Sign Language), how many belong to the Bantu language family?
tasks.notes: Bantu: Ndebele, Northern Sotho (Sepedi), Sotho (Sesotho), Swati, Tsonga, Tswana, Venda, Xhosa, Zulu = 9. Non-Bantu: Afrikaans (IE-Germanic), English (IE-Germanic), South African Sign Language. Filter axis (language family) independent of list axis (official-language status).

tasks.question: Of the 5 most recent UEFA Champions League winning clubs (covering the 2019-20 final through the 2023-24 final inclusive), how many are clubs based in England?
tasks.notes: 2019-20 Bayern Munich (Germany), 2020-21 Chelsea (England), 2021-22 Real Madrid (Spain), 2022-23 Manchester City (England), 2023-24 Real Madrid (Spain). English: Chelsea, Man City = 2.

tasks.question: Of the 5 most recent winners of the Booker Prize for Fiction (2020 through 2024 inclusive), how many were born outside the United Kingdom?
tasks.notes: 2020 Douglas Stuart (Glasgow, UK), 2021 Damon Galgut (South Africa), 2022 Shehan Karunatilaka (Sri Lanka), 2023 Paul Lynch (Ireland), 2024 Samantha Harvey (UK). Outside UK: 3. The 2024 winner is post-cutoff for gpt-4o-mini.

tasks.question: Of the 5 most recent winners of the Eurovision Song Contest (covering 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024 — the 2020 contest was canceled), how many represented a country that is not a member of the European Union?
tasks.notes: 2019 Netherlands (EU), 2021 Italy (EU), 2022 Ukraine (not EU), 2023 Sweden (EU), 2024 Switzerland (not EU). Non-EU: Ukraine, Switzerland = 2. The 2024 winner (Nemo, Switzerland) is post-cutoff for gpt-4o-mini.

tasks.question: Of the 5 most recent Palme d'Or winners at the Cannes Film Festival (covering 2019, 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024 — the 2020 festival was canceled), how many were directed by women?
tasks.notes: 2019 Parasite (Bong Joon-ho, M), 2021 Titane (Julia Ducournau, F), 2022 Triangle of Sadness (Ruben Ostlund, M), 2023 Anatomy of a Fall (Justine Triet, F), 2024 Anora (Sean Baker, M). Women-directed: Ducournau, Triet = 2.

tasks.question: Of the men's Ballon d'Or winners from 2019 through 2024 inclusive (note: the 2020 award was not given), how many were playing for a club based in Spain at the time they received the award?
tasks.notes: 2019 Messi (Barcelona, Spain), 2021 Messi (PSG, France), 2022 Benzema (Real Madrid, Spain), 2023 Messi (Inter Miami, USA), 2024 Rodri (Manchester City, England). Spain-based at time of award: 2019, 2022 = 2. The 2024 winner is post-cutoff for gpt-4o-mini.
